tool_args.label: LIRR & Metro-North Key Performance Metrics - January 2023
tool_args.raw_text: LONG ISLAND RAIL ROAD & METRO-NORTH RAILROAD KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS - January 2023

payload.title: LONG ISLAND RAIL ROAD & METRO-NORTH RAILROAD KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
payload.prepared_for: Joint Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Committees
payload.description: Key performance metrics report for LIRR and Metro-North Railroad prepared for the January 2023 board committee meeting

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA)
tool_args.raw_text: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.

payload.description: North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.

tool_args.label: Long Island Rail Road (LIRR)
tool_args.raw_text: Long Island Rail Road is comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 150,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.

payload.description: Comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 150,000 passengers a day. Operates 700 trains daily and maintains 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.

tool_args.label: Metro-North Railroad
tool_args.raw_text: Metro-North Railroad is comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 150,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

payload.description: Comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 150,000 passengers a day. Operates 700 trains daily and maintains 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

tool_args.label: Cathy Rinaldi
tool_args.raw_text: Cathy Rinaldi, President, Metro-North Railroad, Interim President, Long Island Rail Road

payload.title: President, Metro-North Railroad; Interim President, Long Island Rail Road

tool_args.label: MTA Construction & Development
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction & Development and Metro-North have been hard at work improving behind-the-scenes communications and signal infrastructure on the Harlem Line.

tool_args.label: Grand Central Madison
tool_args.raw_text: Grand Central Madison received customers for the very first time on Wednesday, January 25, when shuttle service from Jamaica kicked off with the 10:45 AM Grand Central Direct train from Jamaica

payload.description: New LIRR terminal beneath Grand Central Terminal, first extension of LIRR service since 1910. Grand Central Direct shuttle service began January 25, 2023 with limited service between Jamaica and Grand Central Madison, operating weekdays 6:15 AM-8:00 PM and weekends 7:00 AM-11:00 PM. Full service to follow with 41% overall service increase.

tool_args.label: Hall Interlocking Expansion Project
tool_args.raw_text: The design-build contract for the Hall Interlocking Expansion Project was just awarded and construction is expected to be completed in the fall of 2026

payload.status: design-build contract awarded, construction expected completion fall 2026
payload.description: Part of JCI Phase 2. A new rail bridge through the center of Jamaica's Hall Interlocking to accommodate two new parallel train routes to increase capacity east of the station.

tool_args.label: Park Avenue Viaduct Phase 1 Replacement & Repair
tool_args.raw_text: The Park Avenue Viaduct is the main artery that serves the heart of the Metro-North system, Grand Central Terminal. Last month, we received board approval of the design-build contract award for Phase 1 partial replacement and repair of the viaduct.

payload.project_name: Park Avenue Viaduct Phase 1 Partial Replacement and Repair
payload.status: design-build contract awarded, major construction expected Q3 2023, completion anticipated 2026
payload.description: Phase 1 partial replacement and repair of the Park Avenue Viaduct. Will replace existing structure, tracks, power, communications, and signal system from north side of E 115th St to south side of E 123rd St. 98% of all Metro-North trains use this viaduct.

tool_args.label: Grand Central Terminal Train Shed Replacement - Sector 1
tool_args.raw_text: The first area to be replaced, called 'Sector 1', is located near JP Morgan Chase (JPMC) headquarters building at 270 Park Avenue.

payload.project_name: Grand Central Terminal Train Shed Replacement Sector 1
payload.status: construction commenced January 2022, street level demolition began January 17, 2023
payload.description: First phase of replacing the GCT Train Shed roof. Collaborative project between MTA and JPMC at 270 Park Avenue. Involves replacing structural framing below Park Avenue between E 43rd-E 57th St and crossing streets between Lexington and Madison Avenues.

tool_args.label: Harlem Express Cable Project
tool_args.raw_text: From Woodlawn Station to Southeast Station – a nearly 42-mile project corridor – the Harlem Express Cable Project replaces communication cables that have reached the end of their useful life.

payload.status: substantial completion at end of January 2023
payload.description: Multi-phase replacement of signal, signal power, and communications infrastructure on Metro-North's Harlem Line from Woodlawn Station to Southeast Station, a nearly 42-mile corridor. Replaces communication cables that have reached end of useful life.

tool_args.label: Cherry Valley Road Bridge Replacement
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction & Development and LIRR are replacing the existing 10'-4" Cherry Valley Road Bridge in Garden City along the Hempstead Branch

payload.status: construction began November 2021, scheduled completion end of 2023
payload.description: Replacement of the Cherry Valley Road Bridge in Garden City along the LIRR Hempstead Branch. New bridge will raise clearance from 10'-4" to 14 feet, meeting NYS DOT standards, ensuring 75-year useful life. $40.8 million project.

tool_args.label: LIRR ADA Station Improvements Bundle (9 stations)
tool_args.raw_text: We are now moving into the design phase of the latest LIRR Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) station improvements bundle in which nine stations (Massapequa Park, Amityville, Copiague, Lindenhurst, Laurelton, Locust Manor, St. Albans, Valley Stream, and Auburndale) will receive accessibility and state-of-good repair upgrades

payload.project_name: LIRR ADA Station Improvements Bundle (9 stations)
payload.status: design phase, work to begin summer 2023, completion roughly 3 years
payload.description: ADA station improvements bundle for Massapequa Park, Amityville, Copiague, Lindenhurst, Laurelton, Locust Manor, St. Albans, Valley Stream, and Auburndale. Includes architectural/electrical improvements, new signage/lighting, ADA travel path upgrades, new elevators at seven stations and replacement elevators at two.

tool_args.label: LIRR ADA Bundle - Forest Hills, Douglaston, Hollis, Cold Spring Harbor
tool_args.raw_text: Design work has already progressed on a separate four-station ADA bundle to make Forest Hills, Douglaston, Hollis and Cold Spring Harbor stations fully accessible as well.

payload.description: Separate four-station ADA bundle to make Forest Hills, Douglaston, Hollis and Cold Spring Harbor stations fully accessible.

tool_args.label: Mott Haven Signal Interlocking Upgrades
tool_args.raw_text: On December 4 in the Mott Haven section of the Bronx, we completed the signal cutover for the first of four critical interlockings in the area that are receiving major communications upgrades.

payload.status: first interlocking cutover completed December 4, 2022; all four anticipated by end of 2023
payload.description: Resiliency program at the convergence of Hudson and Harlem Lines in Mott Haven, Bronx. Upgrading four critical interlockings from 50-year-old analog technology to fiber optic and microprocessor driven control systems. Each interlocking will function independently, eliminating single point of failure.

tool_args.label: Jamaica Capacity Improvements (JCI)
tool_args.raw_text: The Jamaica Capacity Improvements (JCI) project will streamline train routing, enhance service, and increase train throughput capacity as well as train speeds - greatly reducing travel time through the Jamaica complex upon completion.

payload.status: Phase 1 complete, Phase 2 design almost finished
payload.description: Multi-phase project to streamline train routing, enhance service, and increase train throughput capacity and speeds through the Jamaica complex. Phase 1 included Platform F. Phase 2 includes state-of-good-repair work, replacing power/signal systems, extending platforms for 12-car trains, installing higher speed switches.

tool_args.label: Mineola Station Improvements (LIRR Third Track Project)
tool_args.raw_text: Improvements to Mineola Station include new platforms to accommodate 12-car trains, new pedestrian overpass, refurbished station building, platform canopies, new platform furnishings and accoutrements, CCTV security cameras, Wi-Fi, USB charging stations, and digital information displays.

payload.description: Improvements to Mineola LIRR Station as part of the LIRR Third Track Project. Includes new platforms for 12-car trains, new pedestrian overpass, refurbished station building, platform canopies, platform furnishings, CCTV security cameras, Wi-Fi, USB charging stations, and digital information displays. Provides access to Port Jefferson, Ronkonkoma, and Oyster Bay branches.

tool_args.label: Grand Central Madison opens
tool_args.raw_text: Grand Central Madison received customers for the very first time on Wednesday, January 25, when shuttle service from Jamaica kicked off with the 10:45 AM Grand Central Direct train from Jamaica

payload.description: Grand Central Madison received first customers on Wednesday, January 25, 2023, with the 10:45 AM Grand Central Direct train from Jamaica. First extension of LIRR service since 1910.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Grand Central Madison received customers for the very first time on Wednesday, January 25

tool_args.label: Metro-North 40th Anniversary Celebration
tool_args.raw_text: On January 10, it was such a thrill and honor to celebrate 40 years of Metro-North in Grand Central's Vanderbilt Hall

payload.description: 40th anniversary celebration of Metro-North Railroad held at Grand Central's Vanderbilt Hall, with employees past and present including four of the first five presidents.

evidence_refs.source_quote: On January 10, it was such a thrill and honor to celebrate 40 years of Metro-North in Grand Central's Vanderbilt Hall

tool_args.label: "Connect With Us" Event at Grand Central Terminal
tool_args.raw_text: On Tuesday, January 17, we held our first 'Connect With Us' session of 2023 at Grand Central Terminal

payload.description: First 'Connect With Us' session of 2023 at Grand Central Terminal where customers discussed ways of improving service directly with railroad executive staff.

evidence_refs.source_quote: On Tuesday, January 17, we held our first 'Connect With Us' session of 2023 at Grand Central Terminal

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: Mott Haven Signal Cutover - First Interlocking
tool_args.raw_text: On December 4 in the Mott Haven section of the Bronx, we completed the signal cutover for the first of four critical interlockings in the area that are receiving major communications upgrades.

payload.description: Completed signal cutover for the first of four critical interlockings in Mott Haven, Bronx, at the convergence of the Hudson and Harlem Lines.

evidence_refs.source_quote: On December 4 in the Mott Haven section of the Bronx, we completed the signal cutover for the first of four critical interlockings

tool_args.label: "Connect With Us" Event at Grand Central Terminal
tool_args.raw_text: On Tuesday, January 17, we held our first \"Connect With Us\" session of 2023 at Grand Central Terminal which, incidentally, has a big birthday of its own coming up – turning 110 years young on February 2.

payload.description: First 'Connect With Us' session of 2023 at Grand Central Terminal where customers discussed ways of improving service directly with railroad executive staff.

evidence_refs.source_quote: On Tuesday, January 17, we held our first "Connect With Us" session of 2023 at Grand Central Terminal which, incidentally, has a big birthday of its own coming up – turning 110 years young on February 2.

tool_args.label: LIRR total ridership 2022
tool_args.raw_text: Total LIRR ridership for 2022 came-in at 52.5 million customers

tool_args.label: LIRR ridership change 2021 to 2022
tool_args.raw_text: Total LIRR ridership for 2022 came-in at 52.5 million customers, which is 50.1% higher than 2021's total of 35 million

tool_args.label: LIRR 2022 ridership as percent of 2019
tool_args.raw_text: which is 50.1% higher than 2021's total of 35 million and 57.6% of 2019's year-end tally

tool_args.label: Metro-North total ridership 2022
tool_args.raw_text: Total Metro-North ridership for 2022 was 48.9 million

tool_args.label: Metro-North ridership change 2021 to 2022
tool_args.raw_text: Total Metro-North ridership for 2022 was 48.9 million, which was 58.8% higher than 2021's total of 30.8 million

tool_args.label: Metro-North 2022 ridership as percent of 2019
tool_args.raw_text: which was 58.8% higher than 2021's total of 30.8 million and 56.4% of 2019's total

tool_args.label: LIRR commutation ridership increase 2022 vs 2021
tool_args.raw_text: 2022 commutation ridership increased 117% over 2021

payload.raw_value_text: 117% yearly increase in LIRR commutation ridership

tool_args.label: Metro-North commutation ridership increase 2022 vs 2021
tool_args.raw_text: Commutation rose 125.1% over 2021 levels

tool_args.label: Metro-North on-time performance 2022
tool_args.raw_text: 97.1% Metro-North on-time performance rate for 2022

tool_args.label: Metro-North December 2022 on-time performance
tool_args.raw_text: Systemwide, on-time performance was 97.5% and above the goal of 94%

tool_args.label: LIRR December 2022 on-time performance
tool_args.raw_text: For the month of December, on-time performance was 95.5%, above the goal of 94%

payload.raw_value_text: For the month of December, on-time performance was 95.5%

tool_args.label: LIRR 2022 year-to-date on-time performance
tool_args.raw_text: 2022 year-to-date on-time performance is 95.8%, which is above the goal and less than one percent lower than last year

tool_args.label: Metro-North MDBF November 2022
tool_args.raw_text: The mean distance between failures for the fleet was 287,188 miles in November

tool_args.label: LIRR MDBF November 2022
tool_args.raw_text: The fleet mean distance between failures operated at 291,693 miles in November

tool_args.label: Metro-North December 2022 monthly ridership
tool_args.raw_text: In December, Metro-North served 4.61 million customers

tool_args.label: Metro-North December 2022 ridership vs Dec 2021
tool_args.raw_text: a ridership increase of 42% from last December

payload.raw_value_text: a ridership increase of 42% from last December

tool_args.label: Metro-North December 2022 ridership as % of Dec 2019
tool_args.raw_text: representing 62% of December 2019's ridership

tool_args.label: Cherry Valley Road Bridge project cost
tool_args.raw_text: Construction on the $40.8 million project began in November of 2021

tool_args.label: LIRR full GCM schedule service increase
tool_args.raw_text: These new schedules represent a whopping 41% overall service increase

tool_args.label: LIRR ADA stations - 93% accessible target
tool_args.raw_text: once all nine stations are completed roughly three years from now, 93% of our 126 stations will be fully ADA-accessible

payload.claim_text: Once all nine stations are completed roughly three years from now, 93% of our 126 stations will be fully ADA-accessible.
payload.description: LIRR projects that after the 9-station ADA bundle and 4-station ADA bundle are complete, 93% of 126 LIRR stations will be fully ADA-accessible.

tool_args.label: Elmont Station full-time service eventual
tool_args.raw_text: Ridership related to sports and concerts is expected to increase in the future, adding commutation trips when Elmont Station becomes a full-time station once Grand Central Madison service begins

payload.claim_text: Ridership related to sports and concerts is expected to increase in the future, adding commutation trips when Elmont Station becomes a full-time station once Grand Central Madison service begins.
payload.description: Elmont Station expected to become a full-time station once Grand Central Madison service begins, adding commutation trips.
